# Code of Conduct

## Our Pledge

We as members, contributors, and maintainers pledge to make participation in
the smelt project a harassment-free experience for everyone, regardless of
age, body size, visible or invisible disability, ethnicity, sex
characteristics, gender identity and expression, level of experience,
education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race,
religion, or sexual identity and orientation.

## Our Standards

Examples of behavior that contributes to a positive environment:

- Demonstrating empathy and kindness toward other people
- Being respectful of differing opinions, viewpoints, and experiences
- Giving and gracefully accepting constructive feedback
- Focusing on what is best for the community and the project

Examples of unacceptable behavior:

- Harassment, insults, or derogatory comments, public or private
- Trolling, or deliberately disruptive or bad-faith contributions
- Publishing others' private information without explicit permission
- Other conduct which could reasonably be considered inappropriate in a
  professional setting

## Ownership and Responsibility for Contributions

Every contribution to this project — whether typed by hand, generated with
AI assistance, or some mix of the two — is the full responsibility of the
person who opens the pull request and the maintainer who merges it.

- **"An AI wrote it" is never an excuse.** If you submit a PR, you are
  vouching that you have read, understood, and tested the change, regardless
  of what tool produced the diff.
- **AI-assisted contributions must be disclosed** in the PR description
  using the AI Disclosure section of the
  [pull request template](PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md), including the specific
  commit SHA(s) the AI tool produced.
- **Maintainers take responsibility for what they merge.** Approving or
  merging a PR — AI-assisted or not — means the maintainer stands behind the
  change as if they wrote it themselves.

This applies equally to maintainers' own commits: authorship does not
transfer blame or credit away from the human who pushed it.

## Enforcement

Instances of abusive, harassing, or otherwise unacceptable behavior may be
reported to the project maintainer at hardikraina079@gmail.com. All
complaints will be reviewed and investigated promptly and fairly.

## Attribution

This Code of Conduct is adapted from the
[Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/), version 2.1,
with an added section on AI-assisted contribution ownership specific to this
project.
